commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0341edfa1846e25dd0c4fab76f0e24de9fa4c934)
commands.review_summary:
Adds doctor detection for exact ClawHub skill revocation verdicts, a `doctor --fix` quarantine path for verified installed skills, docs/tests, and projection of the new revocation field in ClawHub security verdicts.

PR surface: Source +301, Tests +267, Docs +2. Total +570 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR returns `revocation` from the Gateway verdict projection while the closed Gateway protocol schema lacks that field. I did not run a live doctor reproduction because this is a read-only review and the companion ClawHub API is not deployed per the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer/author follow-up is needed because the remaining blockers include public protocol alignment, companion ClawHub deploy ordering, and contributor-owned real behavior proof that automation cannot provide.

target.title: fix(doctor): quarantine revoked ClawHub skills
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- OpenClaw can install ClawHub skills with exact origin and lock identity, but `doctor` did not consume exact ClawHub revocation verdicts for installed skill versions.

Why does this matter now?

- The companion ClawHub PR adds an exact-version `revocation` field to bulk security verdicts. OpenClaw should treat that explicit signal as a repairable installed-skill health issue.

What is the intended outcome?

- `openclaw doctor` reports installed ClawHub skills whose exact linked version is explicitly revoked.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` moves only lock/origin/path-verified matches out of active skills into `.clawhub/quarantine/skills/` and untracks them from the active lock.
- Offline/API failures, malformed or inconsistent responses, missing revocation fields, and identity/path mismatches never move a skill.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No lock schema migration.
- No irreversible deletion.
- No passive checks for non-default or unconfigured ClawHub registries.

What does success look like?

- Confirmed revoked exact versions are removed from active skill loading by quarantine; every uncertain case leaves the installed skill untouched.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Exact tuple matching, legacy `.clawdhub` lock/origin compatibility, quarantine path containment, and the explicit failure-safe behavior around network/API errors.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

N/A

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related: companion ClawHub PR https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/pull/2495.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

N/A

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: installed ClawHub skill versions with an explicit exact-version revocation verdict are detected by doctor and safely quarantined with `--fix`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree with temp workspace fixtures using the real lifecycle/origin/lock helpers and filesystem operations.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-skills-revoked.test.ts src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: the command passed 4 Vitest shards covering confirmed quarantine, legacy lock/origin compatibility, offline lookup no-op, inconsistent response no-op, lock race no-op, quarantine escape no-op, and active-skills quarantine symlink no-op.
- Observed result after fix: only confirmed exact revoked versions are moved under `.clawhub/quarantine/skills/`; failures and mismatches leave the active skill directory in place.
- What was not tested: a live production ClawHub revoked skill response, because the companion ClawHub API change is not deployed yet.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: remote Testbox/Crabbox submission was not available in this local environment; the changed lanes were validated locally.
- Before evidence: prior `doctor` skill repair only handled unavailable skill requirements and did not query exact ClawHub revocation verdicts.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-skills-revoked.test.ts src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/doctor-skills.ts src/commands/doctor-skills-revoked.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.ts src/skills/security/clawhub-verdicts.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/cli/doctor.md src/infra/clawhub.ts src/skills/security/clawhub-verdicts.ts src/commands/doctor-skills.ts src/commands/doctor-skills-revoked.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm docs:list`
- `corepack pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm check:media-download-helpers`
- `corepack pnpm check:runtime-sidecar-loaders`
- `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles`
- `corepack pnpm lint:webhook:no-low-level-body-read`
- `corepack pnpm lint:auth:no-pairing-store-group`
- `corepack pnpm lint:auth:pairing-account-scope`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/commands/doctor-skills-revoked.test.ts` for confirmed quarantine, legacy compatibility, lookup failure, inconsistent verdicts, lock identity changes, quarantine escape, and quarantine-under-active-skills defenses.
- Updated ClawHub verdict projection coverage to preserve the additive `revocation` object.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The behavior did not exist; installed revoked ClawHub versions were not checked by doctor.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Moving an installed skill directory during `doctor --fix`.

How is that risk mitigated?

The repair only runs after an explicit consistent exact revocation verdict. It revalidates origin, lock identity, installed path, workspace containment, and quarantine containment before rename, then moves to quarantine rather than deleting. API failures and mismatches are no-ops.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft for maintainer review. The companion ClawHub PR should land/deploy first so the additive `revocation` field is available.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer review, CI, and companion ClawHub PR linkage/deploy order.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

N/A
